no_changes_rationale:
Bead: [read-coverage] add ddx_list_personas MCP tool

(a) What is done:
The ddx_list_personas MCP tool is already fully implemented at base-rev
4a3f57cc1040e4a9ab1365bf249b15d7cc9c0641. Concrete evidence:

  - Tool registered in MCP tools/list:
    cli/internal/server/server.go:2869-2878
      Name: "ddx_list_personas"
      Description: "List all available personas"
      InputSchema accepts optional `project` property.

  - Tool dispatch wired in MCP tools/call:
    cli/internal/server/server.go:3486-3487
      case "ddx_list_personas":
          return s.mcpListPersonas(workingDir)

  - Implementation:
    cli/internal/server/server.go:3772-3791  func (s *Server) mcpListPersonas
    Loads via persona.NewPersonaLoader(workingDir).ListPersonas() — the
    SAME loader used by HTTP handleListPersonas at server.go:1140-1142,
    so the JSON shape matches GET /api/personas (name, description,
    roles, tags).

  - Test coverage confirms the tool is registered:
    cli/internal/server/server_test.go:1297 asserts "ddx_list_personas"
    appears in the tools/list response.

  - Git history shows the implementing commit already merged:
    bcdea24b feat(mcp): add ddx_list_personas MCP tool
             [.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-d98d4712]

(b) What is blocking:
Nothing — the AC ("Add ddx_list_personas MCP tool returning the same
data as GET /api/personas") is already satisfied at base-rev. The prior
review's BLOCK appears to have been based on the worktree diff
containing only execution-evidence files, without verifying that the
implementation already existed at the bead's base revision.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need:
No follow-up implementation is required. If the reviewer wants
additional explicit unit-test coverage (e.g. an MCP tools/call test
that invokes ddx_list_personas and asserts the response shape matches
GET /api/personas), that would be a separate bead — it is not part of
the current AC, which only asks that the tool be added.
